title: Isotopes of fluorine
body:
Fluorine (9F) has 19 known isotopes ranging from 13F to 31F and two isomers (18mF and 26mF). Only fluorine-19 is stable and naturally occurring in more than trace quantities; therefore, fluorine is a monoisotopic and a mononuclidic element.
The longest-lived radioisotope is 18F with a half-life of 109.734 minutes, followed by 17F with 64.37 seconds. These unstable isotopes participate in the CNO cycle within stars. All other fluorine isotopes have half-lives of less than 12 seconds, and most of those less than 1/2 second.
